channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: First Friday Craft Night (Apr 17, 2026)
item.description:
We’ll have free crafting and collaging supplies ready for you to play, experiment, and make something uniquely yours. Plus, the City of Raleigh Museum is joining the First Friday fun with extended hours!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Friday, May 1
Time: 6 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Plant Swap (Apr 17, 2026)
item.description:
Bring your favorite house and garden plants or homegrown vegetables to trade with fellow plant enthusiasts. We ask that you bring only healthy, pest-free plants to ensure everyone’s enjoyment. This event is free and open to the public!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, May 23
Time: 11 a.m. - 1 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Rumblin' Tumblers Class at Brier Creek (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
In this class, children will learn the basic building blocks of tumbling, including forward rolls, backward rolls, cartwheels, and handstands. Our experienced instructors will break down these skills into easy-to-follow steps, ensuring your child's safety while they learn. Through a series of playful activities and games, we'll help kids improve their coordination and balance.



Tumbling is an excellent way to boost self-confidence. Children will overcome challenges, achieve goals, and celebrate their accomplishments, fostering a sense of pride in their abilities. We encourage kids to express themselves through movement and creativity. Safety is our top priority, and our dedicated instructors are trained to ensure a safe environment.



Location: Brier Creek Community Center
Date: Mondays
Ages 4-5, 5:15 - 6 p.m.
Ages 6-10, 6:15 - 7 p.m.
Cost: $41
Register on RecLink

item.title: Marsh Creek Park Partners with Raleigh City Farm on New Urban Farm (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
Through this collaboration, the City has strengthened its commitment to community enrichment, agricultural education, and improved food access—further advancing Raleigh Parks’ vision of creating healthy, vibrant spaces for all.



The transformation of 1.5 acres at Marsh Creek Park into a thriving urban farm hub is already making a meaningful impact. The site now features a central growing space and teaching garden, building upon the park’s existing raised beds, pollinator gardens, and edible landscaping. Additional enhancements will include an outdoor prep and teaching area, supporting a wide range of educational and community programs.



Raleigh City Farm, a nonprofit urban farm founded in 2011 in downtown Raleigh, has brought its expertise and mission to “connect and nourish the community through regenerative agriculture” to this new site. The partnership reflects a shared commitment to addressing food insecurity, strengthening local food systems, and creating hands-on learning opportunities in Raleigh’s public spaces.



The recent Gather and Grow: Urban Farm Open House at Marsh Creek Park was a tremendous success, drawing strong community participation and enthusiasm. Attendees toured the site, connected with staff from both organizations, and took home fresh vegetables. The community’s excitement was a great sign for future success.



“It’s my pleasure to welcome the team from Raleigh City Farm into our family at Marsh Creek Park,” said Jamie Quinn. “They bring an incredible amount of passion, knowledge, and experience and we are so thrilled to see how they will help us to cultivate more than just produce – this urban farm partnership will empower people with the skills to grow their own food, increase access to healthy meals, teach people cook, and bring our community closer through shared purpose and passion.”

item.title: MELT Into Summer Wellness at Lake Johnson Park (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
Hosted at the Thomas G. Crowder Woodland Center, these guided sessions offer a simple yet powerful way to support the body’s natural healing processes. The MELT Method is a gentle, hands-off bodywork technique that focuses on the body’s connective tissue, or fascia, to help reduce chronic pain, improve alignment, and restore balance to the nervous system. 

Participants will be guided through easy-to-follow compression and lengthening techniques using a soft foam roller and specialized MELT therapy balls. All equipment is provided, making it easy for beginners to participate.



Participants should be able to get on and off the floor and are encouraged to bring a yoga mat if they have one. Advanced registration is required for all classes. To register, visit RecLink or register in person at any community center.



For those interested in trying the technique before registering, a free drop-in demonstration will be held on Sunday, April 26, from 1 - 3 p.m. at the Lake Johnson Waterfront Center. Attendees can observe, ask questions, or experience the method firsthand.



Location: Thomas G. Crowder Woodland Center
Date and Time: Varies
Free Drop-in Demonstration: Sunday, April 26, 1 - 3 p.m.
Age: 18 and up
Cost: $22 Resident/$37 Non-Resident
Register on RecLink
For additional information, contact Jenny at jenny@foodfityoga.com

item.title: Raleigh Teen Connect Co-Op (Apr 24, 2026)
item.description:
This innovative co-op space is designed for nonprofit agencies serving teenagers and teen-led groups to host unique programs such as educational and career development workshops, recreational activities, and artistic pursuits.



The RTC Co-Op space promotes community and cooperation, allowing different agencies to work alongside one another, share resources, and create a dynamic atmosphere that benefits both the nonprofits and the teenagers they serve. The Raleigh Teen Connect Co-Op aims to provide a centralized hub for organizations to bring their expertise, insights, and creativity, housing a diverse array of opportunities for teenagers in the Raleigh community.



Nonprofit agencies and teen-led groups interested in utilizing the co-op space can apply by completing the interest form. Applications will be accepted May 1 - June 14. Apply Today!

item.title: Live After 5 Hits Moore Square This Summer! (Apr 24, 2026)
item.description:
Join us for free live music, food trucks, and an unforgettable evening downtown. Shows begin at 7 p.m.



Learn more about Live After 5



Location: Moore Square Park
Dates and Time: Select Thursdays at 7 p.m.
Ages: Family-friendly 
Cost: Free

item.title: A New Nash Bash (Apr 23, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh’s historic Nash Square is receiving new love and attention through the Nash Square Public Realm planning project. 



Join us at Nash Square on Saturday, May 30, from 2 to 6 p.m. for a family-friendly public block party, where you can review initial design concepts based on public engagement to date and provide feedback on the future of this vital public space.



The City’s design team, led by Raleigh Parks staff and consultant Reed Hilderbrand, is developing a long-range plan to guide the future of the park and its surrounding edges. The team brings together a wide variety of subject matter experts, city departments, local partners, and communities to envision the future of this shared space.



Our goal is to hear from as many Raleigh residents as possible that live, work, or play in and around Nash Square! 



Location: Nash Square & Whiskey Kitchen 
Date: Saturday, May 30
Time: 2 - 6 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
 



Accommodations and translation services are available upon request. Please contact nashsquare@publicinput.com with details related to the assistance you may need. 



Sign up for project updates and learn more!



Join us all afternoon on Martin Street, between Dawson and McDowell, for live music, food trucks, and interactive tables with City of Raleigh departments and creative expression activities for all ages!



2 - 3 p.m. - Design Presentation and Q&A  at Whiskey Kitchen
Members of the design team will share how initial public input from the Raleigh community informed big ideas featured in the preliminary concept plans.

3 - 6 p.m. - Provide feedback on the design concepts in Nash Square
Meet and talk with project staff, and explore themed walking tours and engagement opportunities.

item.title: Pullen Park Carousel Challenge (Apr 23, 2026)
item.description:
The challenge can be completed all at once or throughout the summer. One ticket is required for each ride around the carousel. Come whirl around the park!



Dates: May 1 - Aug. 31
Time: Pullen Park Amusements Hours
Ages: All
Cost: $2 per ticket
Get Tickets on RecLink

item.title: Pullen Park After Dark (Apr 23, 2026)
item.description:
Each ticket includes one signature mocktail, a ride on the carousel, and the opportunity to make four different hands-on art projects at your leisure. Whether you’re a seasoned crafter or just looking for a relaxing night out, this “paint and sip” offers a creative evening in a unique and historic setting. 

Come in an artistic mood and leave with four different masterpieces of your own. It’s a perfect evening to unleash creative energy, socialize, and a rare opportunity to enjoy Pullen Park after hours.

Your ticket unlocks a full experience: one signature mocktail and the chance to make four unique, hands-on art projects at your own pace. Whether you’re a seasoned maker or just looking for a relaxed night out, each craft station offers something fun, beautiful, or delightfully memorable.



Location: Pullen Park Amusements
Date: Thursday, May 21
Time: 6 - 8:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $40 per person
Get Tickets on RecLink

item.title: Touch-A-Truck at Sanderford Road (Apr 15, 2026)
item.description:
This interactive, family-friendly event allows children and adults to see, touch, and safely explore their favorite trucks and heavy machinery.



Attendees will also have the opportunity to meet the individuals who operate these vehicles while protecting, serving, and improving the Raleigh community.



This event will also include a "No Horn Hour" to ensure that guests with sensitive hearing can comfortably experience the vehicles.



Location: Sanderford Road Park
Date: Saturday, April 25
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m. (No Horn Hour, 11 a.m. - 12 p.m.)
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: First Friday Extended Hours at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum (Apr 15, 2026)
item.description:
Location: Pope House Museum
Date: Fridays starting May 1
Time: 5:30 - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Better Together: How Partnership Fueled a Day of Trail Stewardship (Apr 15, 2026)
item.description:
When an opportunity arose through SAS, the connection was clear—and the collaboration came to life. On April 2, volunteers from SAS and Triangle Trails Initiative gathered at Walnut Creek Wetland Park to support trail restoration. These trails require regular mulching due to the wetland environment and are essential to the park’s nature programming.

Together, each partner played a key role—bringing volunteers, leadership, and expertise to the project. Even on a hot spring day, the group worked hard to improve the trails, creating a lasting impact for park visitors.

Interested in getting involved? Raleigh Parks welcomes new partners to help make a difference in our community.
Partner with Raleigh Parks
Volunteer with Raleigh Parks

item.title: Juneteenth: A Chavis Celebration (Apr 15, 2026)
item.description:
Come together with family, friends, and neighbors for a free day and evening of culture, entertainment, and fun as we honor history and celebrate freedom, resilience and community.



Splash into excitement with waterslides and water games, get creative with art on canvas and hands-on crafts, enjoy a live DJ spinning feel-good tunes, explore vendor booths, participate in interactive activities, take advatage of heath and wellness screenings, cool off at the splash pad, and fuel up with delicious offerings from food trucks.



Keep the fun going with live bands and performances by local artists, dance to DJ-curated music, enjoy interactive activities, face painting, inflatables, and browse vendor booths, all under the evening sky for a festive and memorable night.



Whether you're here to dance, create, connect, or just soak in the festive atmosphere, there's something for everyone. Don't miss this celebration of culture, community, and joy!



Location: John Chavis Memorial Park
Date: Friday, June 19
Time: 10 a.m. - 4 p.m. and 5 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Xtreme STEAM Sports (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
You and your STEAM Team will have a ball learning the science behind the world’s most popular sports while engineering your way through a stadium full of sporting events. From football to NASCAR, from baseball to bobsledding, from tennis to basketball, to the Olympic Games.



Participants should bring snacks, a reusable water bottle, and arrive wearing closed-toed shoes. Program hours are 1 - 4 p.m.



Location: Sanderford Road Neighborhood Center
Date: Monday, July 6 - Friday, July 10
Time: 1 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 7 - 10
Cost: $150
Register on RecLink

item.title: More Trails Coming to Forest Ridge Park (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh City Council authorized the City to accept a $350,000 Great Trails State Program grant to support the Forest Ridge Park Mountain Bike Trail Extension Project.



The project will add 7.3 miles of new trail, expanding mountain biking opportunities in Raleigh. Construction is currently scheduled for completion in 2028.



Learn more and follow project updates on the project webpage

item.title: Life Crafts Camp (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
This half-day camp will teach hands-on life skills, including sewing, cooking, and gardening. These skills will support you through life! Participants should bring a snack and a water bottle. Advance registration is required.



Location: Peach Road Park
Date: Friday, May 1
Time: 8 a.m. - noon
Ages: 7 - 11
Cost: $35 Residents / $50 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks Spring Events and Programs (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 4/7/2026 Updated 4/23/2026



511 South Wilmington Street, Raleigh, NC 27601
Date: Saturdays and Sundays
Time: 10 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $10 per adult / $5 per child
Learn more about the Raleigh Historic Black Neighborhood Walking Tour



1 Mimosa St., Raleigh, NC 27604
Date: Friday and Saturday, April 24 - 25
Time: 8:30 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $3 - $5 per plant
Learn more about the Annual Heirloom Plant Sale



Cranmer Dr, Raleigh, NC 27603
Date: Friday, April 24
Time: 5 - 10 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Learn more about Raleigh Earth Day



911 Ileagnes Rd, Raleigh, NC 27603
Date: Wednesday, April 29
Time: 3:30 - 4:15 p.m.
Ages: 3 - 6
Cost: $7 Residents / $10 Non-residents
Learn more about the Garden Play



511 S. Wilmington St., Raleigh, NC 27601
Date: Saturday, May 9
Time: 2 - 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Learn more about Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Black Joy in the Springtime



7921 Ray Rd, Raleigh, NC 27613
Date: Monday, May 11
Time: 10 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 3 - 5
Cost: Free
Learn more about the Little Sprouts Event at Lake Lynn



Mar 19, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Food Vending Survey (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
We’re always looking for ways to improve the food vending experience in our parks, and your input is an important part of that process.



We’ve created a short survey to gather feedback on your experience vending in Raleigh Parks. Your responses will help us better understand what’s working well and where we can make improvements.



The survey should only take a few minutes to complete. Thank you in advance for sharing your thoughts and helping us enhance our programs and services.



Take the Survey  



Questions, please email adam.forman@raleighnc.gov

item.title: Raleigh Earth Day (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
This family-friendly event brings together community members, local organizations, and city departments for an evening dedicated to environmental stewardship, sustainability, and connection—all in one of Raleigh’s most beautiful public spaces.

Explore earth-conscious vendors, enjoy live performances and hands-on activities, celebrate local environmental leaders, and end the night with an inspirational film on the big screen under the stars.

The event emphasizes low-waste practices and practical ways to care for our planet every day. 



Help support Dix Park event staff, ensuring everyone has a fun and safe experience, and the park is clean and green after the event!
Sign up and learn more about volunteering



When: Friday, April 24, 2026, 5 - 10 p.m.
Where: Harvey Hill at Dix Park - Cranmer Dr, Raleigh, NC 27603

item.title: Lost Raleigh: Exploring the Oak City's Architectural History Book Launch (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description: This book is a comprehensive look at the city’s cultural and architectural past and a plea to protect and preserve the landmarks that make our cities unique, connecting us to our shared history. Grab your copy, get it personally signed, and walk away with an exclusive, limited edition bookmark absolutely free.

item.title: Big Screen Bash (Apr 22, 2026)
item.description:
Families will enjoy a variety of competitive and carnival-style games, including tug-of-war, relay races, classic carnival games, and bingo. 

As the sun sets, folks will gather in the gym for a showing of Zootopia 2 on the big screen, complete with drinks and snacks to close out the evening in comfort and style! 

Be sure to bring chairs, blankets, and your own food and beverages. Drinks and snacks provided will be limited. Spots are limited!



Location: Lions Park Community Center
Date: Friday, May 29
Time: 7 - 10 p.m.
Age: All
Cost: $5
Register on RecLink

item.title: 2026 Lake Wheeler Invitational (Apr 22, 2026)
item.description:
Park Closure Notice: Boat Ramps will be closed on April 22. Lake Wheeler Park will be closed to the public from Thursday, April 23, through Saturday, April 25.




Spectators are encouraged to come out and experience high-level collegiate rowing competition and support these outstanding student-athletes. For questions or additional information, please contact Lake Wheeler Park Office at 919-996-6740

item.title: REFUGE Film Screening and Panel (Apr 14, 2026)
item.description:
The film follows a former white nationalist leader as he begins to confront his beliefs after connecting with a Muslim refugee doctor and the community in Clarkston, Georgia. Through their experiences, REFUGE looks at the roots of hate and what it can take to move past it. 

The screening will be followed by a discussion with local and national experts on how people and communities work through division, build trust, and find common ground.



Panelists include:



Doors open at 6:15 p.m., and the event begins at 6:30 p.m. Heavy snacks will be provided.

This program is presented in partnership with We Choose Welcome and was financially supported by Welcome.US. It is presented in anticipation of the June opening of Patterns of Belonging: English Learners’ Reflections on America 250.

item.title: Many Ways. One Greenway. (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Are you the cyclist? The walker/runner? The nature observer? The outdoor adventurer? Pick the one that feels like you, and let us know how you use the greenway.



From April to May, you'll see friendly reminders along the trails about etiquette on our greenways, and you may run into our Raleigh Parks Ambassadors with some fun giveaway items and activities!



Take the quiz about how you use the greenway to receive a discount on your next Raleigh Parks program.

item.title: Free Suicide Prevention Training (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness, in partnership with the QPR Institute will host a free Question, Persuade, Refer (QPR) Training on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, from 6 to 8 p.m. at Jaycee Park Community Center (2405 Wade Ave, Raleigh, NC 27607).



This event is open to all North Carolina residents and is designed to provide suicide prevention knowledge and skills. 



Just as people trained in CPR and the Heimlich Maneuver help save thousands of lives each year, people trained in QPR learn how to recognize the warning signs of a suicide crisis and how to question, persuade, and refer someone to help.



Date: Wednesday, May 13
Time: 6 - 8 p.m. 
Location: Jaycee Community Center (2405 Wade Ave, Raleigh, NC 27607)
Registration: QPR Training- Jaycee  



Key Information: 



 Content Notice: This training includes discussion about suicide and mental health. These topics may be distressing for some individuals. If you or someone you know is in crisis, support is available by calling or texting 988, the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline.

item.title: Hiking for Beginners (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Learn the basics of hiking on a 3-mile hike with an experienced instructor. You'll learn how to plan a hike, what to bring, and what to wear.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Date: Sunday, May 3
Time: 9 - 11 a.m.
Age: 14 and older
Cost: $6
Register on RecLink

item.title: Annual International Children's Day (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Meet these incredible North Carolina creators, hear them read from their award-winning books, and get your copies personalized



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, May 30 
Time: 10 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Introduction to Camping (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
This program will provide a wealth of knowledge for new campers or those planning and leading group trips. We will walk you through the basics of camping in four instruction sessions. The sessions will cover finding a place to camp, navigating online reservation systems, campsite rules, and what to do when you get there. 



You will learn about camp cooking, outdoor ethics, first aid, camp safety, and what to do in an emergency. After the sessions, our instructors will lead a camping trip to practice your skills at Durant Nature Preserve, Aug. 29 - 30. You must attend 3 of the 4 instruction sessions to participate in the campout. Please contact Forest Ridge Park for more information.



Instruction sessions: First Thursday of every month (5/7, 6/4, 7/2, 8/6), 5:30 - 7 p.m.
Campout at Durant: Aug. 29 - 30
Ages: Sessions -18 and older/ Campout - Family/All ages
Cost: $65 resident /$80 non-resident
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness Pop-Up Fitness Classes (Apr 21, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness will host a variety of fitness and mindfulness classes throughout May at the Glen Eden Pilot Park Facility. Come join us! 



These classes are open to all North Carolina residents and are designed to provide health and wellness activities for the community, ranging from strength training to yoga. 



Location: Glen Eden Pilot Park
Dates and Times: Vary
Ages: 18 and older for most classes 
Cost: $5 - $10

item.title: What’s Next for Forestville Road Park? (Apr 21, 2026)
item.description:
Shaped by community input, the plan provides a shared vision for how the park will serve the community in the years ahead.  



To guide the development of the park, four design goals were established: Honor History, Foster Community, Conserve Nature, and Inspire Play. These design goals expand on the vision statement for the park: Rooted in history and built for community, Forestville Road Park will be a place for exploration, nature, and play.



While there is no immediate timeline for construction, adoption of the master plan is an important milestone that helps position the project for future funding and development.



Learn more and view the master plan on the project webpage.

item.title: Adventure Outdoors: Teen Camps (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
It's bike week, come along for the ride. This camp is for teens looking to expand their biking skills or seasoned shredders. Join us as we learn new maneuvers, practice techniques, and experience new trails. This camp will focus on riding single-track trails starting at Forest Ridge Park and taking field trips to ride some of the areas best biking trails. This camp is scheduled to be action-packed, with most of our time being in the outdoors. Don't have a bike, don't worry. We can provide all equipment necessary to fully participate, including bikes and helmets. Program times are from 9 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. Drop off starts at 8:30 a.m. and pick up lasts until 5:15 p.m. (Friday is a half-day)



Dates: Monday, July 22 - Friday, July 26
Time:  8:30 a.m. - 5:15 p.m.
Cost: $$475 Resident / $490 Non-Resident
Ages: 13 - 15 years
Register on RecLink

item.title: Register Now for May Adult Technology Classes (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
Digital Inclusion Programs



News Published 4/13/2026 Updated 4/14/2026



Classes are only $6-$12 and meet at one of our Centers for Active Adults.



Mondays, May 4 and 11, 10 - 11:30 a.m.
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $12
Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults



Whether you're brand new to using an Android phone or just want to sharpen your skills, this session is your gateway to mastering your device with confidence.



Learn more about Android Phone Basics 1
Register on RecLink



Tuesdays,  May 5 and  12, 10 a.m. - 11:30 am
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $12
Five Points Center for Active Adults



Whether you're just getting started with a computer or brushing up on the basics, this course will help you get started with Windows 11. From navigating your desktop to managing your internet connection, you'll gain confidence in using key features that make your digital life smoother and more productive.



Learn more about Computer Basics 1
Register on RecLink



Tuesdays,  May 5 and 12,  2 - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $12
Five Points Center for Active Adults



Whether you're brand new to the iPhone or just looking to sharpen your skills, this course is your perfect starting point. We’ll walk you through the essential features that make your iPhone a powerful, intuitive tool for everyday life. Gain the confidence to start using your device.



Learn more about iPhone Basics 1
Register on RecLink



Wednesday,  May 6, 10 a.m. - noon
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $6
Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults



Learn how to get car rides on demand with budget-friendly options using apps on your smartphone! It is an easy way to give you freedom to get around at your convenience - go anywhere you want to go - when you want to do it! Plus, learn how to get food delivered from your favorite restaurants and grocery stores to your home. All through the convenience of your smartphone and the Internet!



Learn more about Rides, Meals, and Groceries On‑Demand
Register on RecLink



Wednesdays, May 6 and 13, 2 - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $12
Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults



"Cutting the Cord" is a phrase often associated with steps to lower your television or cable bill. But how would you even begin? Learn the steps some people are taking to cut the cord. But at the end of the day, are you really “Cutting the Cord” or simply paying more for television on a different platform?



Learn more about Cutting the Cord
Register on RecLink



Thursdays,  May 7 and  14,  10 - 11:30 a.m.
Ages: 18 and older, Cost: $12
Five Points Center for Active Adults



Explore a powerful image editing tool without the hefty software price tag! Unlock the full potential of your creativity with Gimp, an image editing program designed for photo retouching, compositing, and graphic design.



Learn more about Gimp Photo Editing Basics 1
Register on RecLink



Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults
Five Points Center for Active Adults



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Falls Lake Paddle Tours (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
These 2-hour paddle trips are along the park's scenic northern shoreline. We will meet at the waterfront, go over basic instructions, and launch together. Guidance and all necessary paddle equipment are provided.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Dates and Times: Vary
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $30 resident / $45 non-resident
Register on RecLink

item.title: NCMA’s Outdoor Movie Series Lights Up Moore Square This Spring and Summer (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
Join us monthly for themed activities and programming, including food trucks beginning at 6 p.m., followed by an evening film under the stars at 7:15 pm.



Location: Moore Square
Food Trucks: 6 - 9 p.m.
Movie: 7:15 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Presented in partnership with the Downtown Raleigh Alliance.

item.title: First Friday Market and Movie Night Returns for 2026 (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
This free monthly event brings the community together for local vendors, food trucks, live music, and a featured outdoor movie in the heart of downtown Raleigh.



Join us on the first Friday of each month from May through November, with the market and food trucks beginning at 5 p.m., live music at 5:30 p.m., and the movie starting around 7:15 p.m.



Market & Food Trucks: 5 - 9 p.m.
Live Music: 5:30 p.m.
Movie:  7:15 p.m.*
Ages: All
Cost: Free



Presented in partnership with Get 2 Know Raleigh. Movie sponsored by the Downtown Raleigh Alliance.

item.title: All-Sports Class at Brier Creek! (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
All-Sports introduces young children to a new sport each week, including basketball, football, soccer, and more! Each session will teach the basic fundamental skills for each sport.



This class will also teach how to be a good teammate, sportsmanship, and confidence! Join us and make every kid feel like a champion! All equipment will be provided.



Location: Brier Creek Park
Date: Tuesdays
Time: 5:30 p.m.
Ages: 4 - 6 years old
Cost: $41
Register on RecLink

item.title: Little Sluggers at Brier Creek! (Apr 13, 2026)
item.description:
Are you looking to introduce your child to baseball or softball, but not quite ready to sign up for a full season/league yet? Then Little Sluggers is the class for you!



This class will introduce and teach the basic fundamentals of baseball/softball. Bring your own glove, but all other equipment will be provided. Please make sure to wear comfortable clothes.



Location: Brier Creek Park
Date: Tuesdays
Time: 4:30 p.m.
Ages: 4 - 6 years old
Cost: $41
Register on RecLink

item.title: Slushy Saturdays at John Chavis Memorial Park (Apr 20, 2026)
item.description:
Slushy Saturdays bring bright colors, bold flavors, and icy-cold fun to John Chavis Memorial Park. A food truck will be on-site for cold treat purchases.



Date: Saturdays starting May 2 - Aug 29
Time: Noon - 4 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Shakespeare in the Parks: As You Like It (Apr 20, 2026)
item.description:
All the world’s a stage! This fourth annual free-to-the-public event brings Shakespeare’s madcap story of love, mistaken identity, and finding oneself, As You Like It, to five locations around the Triangle. When the spirited Rosalind is exiled by her usurping uncle, she dons a male disguise and flees to the forest, only to find it crawling with fellow refugees—including her crush, the farmer-poet Orlando. 

What follows is a masterclass in romantic chaos as Rosalind, acting as "Ganymede," mentors a lovesick Orlando in the art of wooing while juggling mismatched shepherds and searching for her long-lost father. Through wit, bad poetry, and high-stakes matchmaking, the group of outcasts proves that reconciliation is possible, and that finding love is the greatest adventure of all. Set in a fictionalized North Carolina and infused with local music, this abridged version is the perfect way to experience the magic of Shakespeare under the open sky or on your busy schedule. 

Tickets are free, but reservations are recommended.



Location: Fred Fletcher Park
Dates and Times: 
Saturday, May 16, 6 p.m.
Sunday, May 17, 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Tickets are free, but reservations are recommended.
Reserve Your Tickets

Ages: All

item.title: Raleigh Community Health Fair! (Apr 20, 2026)
item.description:
This is a free event dedicated to supporting the health and well-being of our community!



Here’s what awaits you:



In addition, community members can participate in a blood drive, and donors will receive a gift card as a thank you! This is a wonderful opportunity to access important health services, connect with local organizations, and enjoy a day of community support, all at no cost. Hope to see you there!



Location: Roberts Park
Date: Saturday, April 25
Time: 10 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Warm Season Lawncare for Beginners (Apr 10, 2026)
item.description:
Millbrook Exchange Park



News Published 4/10/2026 Updated 4/10/2026



Whether you're just getting started or looking to elevate your lawn care game, this class will cover everything you need to know to keep your yard lush and thriving year-round. Join us as we break down fertilizers, pre-emergents, mowing, pest control, and more!



Take home a lawn care calendar, tips for sustainable lawn practices, and the confidence to transform your lawn into a neighborhood showpiece! Receive expert guidance tailored specifically for warm-season grasses, including Bermuda, Zoysia, Centipede, and St. Augustine.



Location: Millbrook Exchange
Dates: Wednesday, May 6
Time: 6:15 - 8 p.m.
Ages: 15 and older
Cost: $15
Register on RecLink



Millbrook Exchange Park
1905 Spring Forest Rd.
Raleigh, NC 27615



919-996-4156



Millbrook Exchange Park



Apr 7, 2026



Feb 3, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: More than $1 Million Secured to Restore Rocky Branch at Dix Park (Apr 2, 2026)
item.description:
Rocky Branch Creek is on its way to becoming a nature escape in the heart of Raleigh.  



Congresswoman Deborah Ross joined Mayor Janet Cowell and City leaders to announce $1,092,000 in federal funding for the Rocky Branch Restoration.  



The project opens up more public space for residents to connect with nature. Visitors will explore restored creek banks, spot native plants and wildlife, and enjoy new pathways, boardwalks, and shaded seating areas. Once complete, Dix Park will have a connected green space between Downtown Raleigh and Gipson Play Plaza. 



These improvements aren’t just for people to enjoy. The project helps prevent flooding by giving the creek room to hold stormwater. It will also restore plants and habitat to improve water quality for Dix Park and downstream neighbors.
